content.Text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 05:00 PDT] Create docs/layout-optimizer.html — an interactive Graph Layout Optimizer tool. This is a Java graph visualization project; all interactive tools live as standalone HTML files in docs/.

Look at an existing file like docs/matching.html or docs/dominating-set.html for the style/structure pattern. Follow the same HTML structure, CSS theming (dark theme with accent colors), and self-contained single-file approach.

## Feature: Autonomous Graph Layout Optimizer

An interactive tool that automatically optimizes graph layouts using simulated annealing to minimize edge crossings and improve visual clarity.

### Core functionality:
1. **Canvas-based graph editor** — click to add nodes, click two nodes to add edges, drag nodes to move them
2. **8 preset graphs** — Petersen, K5, K3,3, Cube, Dodecahedron, Grid 4x4, Random(15,25), Bipartite(5,5)
3. **Simulated Annealing optimizer** — autonomously improves layout by minimizing a cost function:
   - Edge crossing count (primary)
   - Edge length variance (secondary)
   - Node-node proximity penalty (tertiary)
   - Angular resolution bonus
4. **Real-time animation** — watch the optimizer work, nodes moving as it finds better layouts
5. **Quality metrics dashboard** — show current: crossings, edge length std dev, min node distance, angular resolution score, overall quality score (0-100)
6. **Controls**: Start/Stop/Reset buttons, temperature slider, cooling rate slider, speed slider
7. **Comparison mode** — show before/after side by side with metric improvements
8. **Auto-optimize on load** — when a preset is selected, auto-start optimization (can be toggled)
9. **Text input** — paste adjacency list to define custom graphs

### Quality metrics panel should update in real-time during optimization showing:
- 🔀 Crossings: N
- 📏 Edge Length σ: X.XX  
- 📐 Angular Resolution: X.XX°
- 📊 Quality Score: XX/100
- 🌡️ Temperature: X.XX
- ⚡ Iterations: N

### Technical notes:
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies
- Use Canvas API for rendering
- Simulated annealing: random node displacement, accept if cost decreases or with probability e^(-ΔE/T)
- Edge crossing detection: line segment intersection test
- Smooth animation with requestAnimationFrame
- Dark theme consistent with other tools in the project

Make it self-contained in a single HTML file. The file should be production-ready and polished.
I’m inspecting the existing `docs/` tools first so the new file matches the project’s single-file structure, theming, and control patterns before I build the optimizer.

👑 Dominating Set Finder Find minimum dominating sets with greedy & exact algorithms

A dominating set S of a graph G is a subset of vertices such that every vertex is either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Finding a minimum dominating set is NP-hard, but greedy approximation works well in practice. This tool lets you visualize both approaches step by step.

About Dominating Sets

📡

Wireless Networks

Dominating sets model optimal placement of relay nodes — every device is within one hop of a relay.

🏥

Facility Location

Place facilities (hospitals, fire stations) so every community is adjacent to at least one.

🔒

Network Monitoring

Position sensors to observe all network links — a dominating set monitors every neighbor.

🧬

Biological Networks

Identify essential proteins in PPI networks: a dominating set covers all interaction partners.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

💍 Maximum Matching Finder Find maximum matchings with augmenting path visualization

A matching in a graph is a set of edges with no shared vertices. A maximum matching has the largest possible number of edges. This tool visualizes three algorithms: a simple Greedy heuristic, the Augmenting Path (Berge) method for general graphs, and Hopcroft-Karp for bipartite graphs.
